name: usd_10y_cms_floorlet_atm_hagan_ref
description: Single-period ATM CMS-10Y floorlet priced by static replication. Reference values from Hagan (2003) first-order convexity-adjusted Black-76 formula using the discrete semi-annual annuity. Locks the Task-8 sign fix at the JSON-framework level.

provenance.source_detail: Hagan (2003) first-order convexity-adjustment approximation for a CMS floorlet. All quantities derived analytically; no circular dependency on codebase output. Derivation summary: (1) Flat continuous OIS curve r=4%, DF(t)=exp(-0.04t); (2) T=367/365 Act365F years (2026-05-16 to 2027-05-18); (3) A0=sum_{k=1}^{20} 0.5*exp(-0.04*(T+0.5k))=7.83818301, F_swap=(exp(-0.04T)-exp(-0.04*(T+10)))/A0=0.04040268; (4) Hagan discrete semi-annual convexity adjustment via d^2(1/A)/dS^2=1.58781176: C_adj=0.5*0.04*T*F^2*1.58781=0.000052122 (0.52 bps); (5) F_cms=0.04045480, K=F_swap=0.04040268; (6) Black76_put(F_cms,K,0.20,T)=0.0032032; (7) V=1e6*exp(-0.04T)*0.0032032=3076.90 USD; (8) DV01 via bumping r+/-1bp in the same Hagan formula=-45.26 USD/bp. References: Hagan (2003) Wilmott Magazine Mar pp.38-44; Andersen & Piterbarg (2010) Interest Rate Modeling Vol.1 section 16.2; Brigo & Mercurio (2006) Interest Rate Models 2nd ed. section 13.7.

source_reference.note: Reference values derived analytically from the Hagan (2003) first-order convexity-adjusted Black-76 formula using the discrete semi-annual par annuity. The static replication integral (Andersen-Piterbarg section 16.2) executed by the Rust runner is an independent, more accurate method expected to differ from this Hagan first-order reference by approximately 5% (NPV) and up to 15% (DV01) due to higher-order convexity corrections. Tighter sign-fix regression is provided by tests/instruments/cms_option/replication_sign.rs.

plan.description: Flat 4% continuous OIS discount curve (Act365F log-linear) and flat 20% lognormal vol surface carried verbatim in prior_market. Empty plan.steps: no calibration, market snapshot used directly. Single-curve setup: discount_curve_id = forward_curve_id = USD-FLAT-4PCT so that the forward swap rate equals the par rate of the flat-curve swap exactly (no multi-curve basis).

npv.tolerance_reason: Reference is the Hagan (2003) first-order convexity-adjustment approximation (C_adj=0.52 bps, V_ref=3076.90 USD per 1M notional). The executable uses the exact static replication integral (Andersen-Piterbarg section 16.2) capturing all higher-order corrections absent from the first-order Hagan formula. For a 10Y CMS floorlet with sigma=20%, T=1Y, the first-order approximation error is numerically approximately 5% relative (numerical replication integral gives approximately 2916 USD, Hagan gives 3077 USD). The 10% tolerance bounds this with a factor-of-2 safety margin. Tighter sign-fix regression is provided by tests/instruments/cms_option/replication_sign.rs.

dv01.tolerance_reason: DV01 reference is a numerical central-difference of the Hagan first-order formula at +/-1bp: -45.26 USD/bp. The static replication DV01 inherits the same relative approximation error as the NPV (~5-10%), amplified for the derivative. The 15% relative tolerance accounts for this. Sign: negative DV01 means PV increases when rates fall, correct for a receiver floorlet.
